jokes.text: I met him after Princeton.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil claims playing trumpet in college helped him with the girls
jokes.punchline: Vivian dryly reveals she met him after college, deflating his story
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan timing and character moment. Vivian's dry wit deflates Phil's boastful story with surgical precision and minimal words.

jokes.text: Well, it's not mine. My Barbie's at home fast asleep in her Barbie Dream House.
jokes.setup: Someone questions who owned the Barbie doll found in the attic
jokes.punchline: Carlton defensively reveals he has his own Barbie at home, with elaborate details
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Carlton's character - his defensiveness and the absurd specificity of 'fast asleep in her Barbie Dream House' makes this both endearing and hilarious.

jokes.text: Um... It doesn't matter whose it is. Let me see this trumpet.
jokes.setup: Carlton's Barbie revelation creates uncomfortable silence
jokes.punchline: Phil awkwardly tries to change the subject, his discomfort obvious
jokes.explanation: Standard reaction beat. Phil's discomfort is relatable and well-timed but not particularly memorable.

jokes.text: Uh... Uh... Uh... Billy. Billy who? Uh... Uh... Uh... Uh... Billy Butterworth.
jokes.setup: Carlton asks who's coming on the show, Will tries to cover up William Shatner
jokes.punchline: Will's obvious panic and the ridiculous fake name 'Billy Butterworth'
jokes.explanation: Will Smith's physical comedy shines through the stammering. The fake name is absurd enough to be funny but not groundbreaking.

jokes.text: Actor, director, novelist, bon vivant William Shatner?
jokes.setup: Carlton learns William Shatner is coming on the show
jokes.punchline: Carlton's over-the-top reverent listing of Shatner's credentials including 'bon vivant'
jokes.explanation: Pure Carlton - his pretentious vocabulary ('bon vivant') and reverent tone perfectly capture his character while being genuinely funny.

jokes.text: You slept in the same pool house with me knowing this... knowing what it would mean to me, and you didn't say a word? I don't even know who you are.
jokes.setup: Carlton realizes Will knew about Shatner and didn't tell him
jokes.punchline: Carlton's melodramatic betrayal speech ending with 'I don't even know who you are'
jokes.explanation: Carlton's dramatic overreaction to this 'betrayal' is perfectly in character. The escalating intensity and final devastating line land perfectly.

jokes.text: Hello? I wasn't finished.
jokes.setup: Will tries to interrupt Carlton's dramatic speech
jokes.punchline: Carlton imperiously demands to finish his melodramatic moment
jokes.explanation: Carlton's self-important demand to finish his dramatic speech shows his theatrical nature. Good character beat.

jokes.text: Okay. What do you mean 'okay?' ... Okay. What...?
jokes.setup: Will expresses concern about losing his job, Hilary responds dismissively
jokes.punchline: Hilary's repeated casual 'Okay' responses to Will's legitimate concerns
jokes.explanation: Establishes Hilary's characteristic self-absorption and indifference to others' problems. Sets up a running gag for the episode.

jokes.text: Oh, don't tell me someone withheld information from somebody.
jokes.setup: Will complains about not knowing about the New York move in advance
jokes.punchline: Carlton sarcastically references Will withholding the Shatner information
jokes.explanation: Nice callback that shows Carlton's pettiness. The sarcastic delivery works well.

jokes.text: Ashley, you've never been this happy for me. I still haven't been. This is about me.
jokes.setup: Hilary thinks Ashley is finally happy for her success
jokes.punchline: Ashley bluntly clarifies she's happy about her own opportunity, not Hilary's
jokes.explanation: Sharp misdirection that perfectly captures both characters - Hilary's narcissism and Ashley's honest directness.

jokes.text: Perhaps that iron fist that you rule with has gotten a bit rusty, sir.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil is losing control of the Ashley situation
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's polite but cutting observation about Phil's parenting
jokes.explanation: Geoffrey's formal, polite way of delivering cutting observations is perfectly in character. The 'rusty iron fist' metaphor is clever.

jokes.text: Perhaps I should hit something.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey's comment about Phil's rusty iron fist
jokes.punchline: Phil's matter-of-fact suggestion that he should hit something
jokes.explanation: Phil's deadpan threat works with his character's occasional hints of violence. Brief but effective.

jokes.text: Be careful of misguided anger, sir.
jokes.setup: Phil threatens to hit something
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's calm, therapeutic advice delivered with butler formality
jokes.explanation: Geoffrey being a wise counselor while maintaining butler formality is a nice character beat.

jokes.text: Okay.
jokes.setup: Will gives an emotional speech about being hurt by her actions
jokes.punchline: Hilary's dismissive 'Okay' to his heartfelt concerns
jokes.explanation: Continues the running gag of Hilary's indifference. Simple but effective callback.

jokes.text: Thought beaming down would be too flashy, huh? Good one, Dick.
jokes.setup: Network executive makes obvious Star Trek reference to Shatner
jokes.punchline: Shatner's polite but clearly annoyed response to the cliché joke
jokes.explanation: Meta-joke about how people always make the same Star Trek references to Shatner. His weary tolerance is relatable.

jokes.text: What is it about these suits that they feel they have to make some lame Star Trek joke when they start off?
jokes.setup: Shatner just dealt with another lame Star Trek reference
jokes.punchline: His direct commentary on the predictable pattern of executives
jokes.explanation: Smart meta-commentary that breaks the fourth wall slightly. Shows awareness of the predictable nature of celebrity interactions.

jokes.text: I guess you thought beaming down would be too flashy, huh? Get it, huh? That's a good one, Dick. Well, actually, his name is Carlton. Who cares?
jokes.setup: Carlton makes the exact same Star Trek joke as the executive
jokes.punchline: Shatner's identical response followed by dismissive 'who cares' about Carlton's name
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback that shows both the predictability of fan interactions and Shatner's growing irritation. The 'who cares' dismissal is perfectly cruel.

jokes.text: That's a good one, Dick. / Well, actually, his name is Carlton. / Who cares?
jokes.setup: Shatner gives the same fake response to Carlton's joke
jokes.punchline: When corrected about Carlton's name, Shatner dismissively says 'Who cares?'
jokes.explanation: Shatner's casual cruelty and indifference. His dismissive 'Who cares?' about Carlton's name is perfectly rude celebrity behavior.

jokes.text: How about that Heather Locklear? Hubba, hubba.
jokes.setup: Carlton is trying to bond with Shatner over his career
jokes.punchline: Carlton's awkward 'hubba hubba' comment about Shatner's T.J. Hooker co-star
jokes.explanation: Carlton's outdated 'hubba hubba' expression and awkward attempt at male bonding is perfectly cringeworthy.

jokes.text: Yeah, get away from this guy.
jokes.setup: Will asks Shatner what he wants to do for three hours
jokes.punchline: Shatner bluntly states he wants to get away from Carlton
jokes.explanation: Brutally honest response that shows how much Carlton has annoyed Shatner. The directness is both cruel and funny.

jokes.text: Warm? It doesn't get that cold in California.
jokes.setup: Someone comments that Ashley's winter gear will keep her warm
jokes.punchline: Hilary's confused response about California weather for New York gear
jokes.explanation: Classic Hilary confusion showing her airheaded nature. She doesn't understand why Ashley needs winter gear.

jokes.text: I remember my first visit to New... Mexico. Ah. New Mexico. Heh. Nothing like taking a bite of the Big Ap... Tostada.
jokes.setup: Vivian starts to share a New York memory but catches herself
jokes.punchline: Her awkward correction to New Mexico and 'Big Tostada' instead of Big Apple
jokes.explanation: Vivian's attempt to avoid encouraging Ashley's New York dreams leads to an awkward cover-up. The 'Big Tostada' wordplay is clever.

jokes.text: You are not going to New York. Vivian. Vi... She's not going to New York. You are not going to New York. Did you hear...? I'm your fath... You're not going to New York.
jokes.setup: Phil is losing the argument about Ashley moving to New York
jokes.punchline: His increasingly desperate and fragmented protests as he loses control
jokes.explanation: Phil's parental desperation building to a breaking point. The fragmented speech shows him losing his usual composure.

jokes.text: She's not going to New York.
jokes.setup: Phil's continued protestations
jokes.punchline: His defeated final statement, probably to an empty room
jokes.explanation: The final beat of Phil's denial. Shows his defeat but not particularly memorable.

jokes.text: Most people are intimidated by me. They let me win.
jokes.setup: Context of playing pool with Will
jokes.punchline: Shatner's arrogant assumption that people let him win out of intimidation
jokes.explanation: Shows Shatner's ego and sets up the irony that he might not be as good as he thinks.

jokes.text: Very funny, Will, giving me the wrong address.
jokes.setup: Carlton arrives at what was supposed to be Shatner's location
jokes.punchline: Reveals Will deliberately gave Carlton wrong directions
jokes.explanation: Reveals Will's attempt to keep Carlton away from Shatner. Good setup for Carlton finding them anyway.

jokes.text: All right, I won't talk. I promise. And? And if I feel myself getting overexcited, I take a time out.
jokes.setup: Will sets rules for Carlton to stay near Shatner
jokes.punchline: Carlton's childish agreement to rules like he's being disciplined
jokes.explanation: Carlton agreeing to be treated like an overexcitable child around his idol is both pathetic and funny.

jokes.text: Oh, I don't need a time out. I don't need a time out. I'm sorry, Will, I can't help it.
jokes.setup: Carlton promised to take a time out if he got overexcited
jokes.punchline: He immediately breaks his promise and loses control
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback showing Carlton can't control himself around Shatner. His internal struggle is relatable and funny.

jokes.text: Just make it out to, 'Carlton, my best friend on Earth, bar none.'
jokes.setup: Carlton asks Shatner to sign a picture
jokes.punchline: His ridiculously specific and presumptuous autograph request
jokes.explanation: Carlton's delusional belief that he and Shatner are best friends is perfectly cringeworthy. The specificity of 'bar none' makes it funnier.

jokes.text: I can't. I have this arrangement with the people who do the Star Trek conventions. I have to charge you $50.
jokes.setup: Carlton wants a free autograph from his 'best friend'
jokes.punchline: Shatner coldly explains he has to charge convention prices
jokes.explanation: Brilliant deflation of Carlton's fantasy. Shatner treating him like any other fan despite Carlton's belief they're friends.

jokes.text: Try and cash it at the bar. And while you're there, get me a drink.
jokes.setup: Carlton offers to pay by check for the autograph
jokes.punchline: Shatner dismisses the check and treats Carlton like a servant
jokes.explanation: Shatner's casual cruelty escalates. He's treating Carlton like hired help rather than a fan.

jokes.text: Carlton? No, no, no. Not him. I mean, he cute but I ain't never really...
jokes.setup: Shatner asks about Will's attractive cousin, Will misunderstands
jokes.punchline: Will's confused response suggesting romantic thoughts about Carlton
jokes.explanation: Will's momentary confusion creates an awkward beat. The humor comes from the misunderstanding.

jokes.text: Better watch it. Hilary be taking aerobics and stuff like that. It might hurt you.
jokes.setup: Shatner expresses interest in Hilary
jokes.punchline: Will warns that Hilary's fitness routine might be dangerous for Shatner
jokes.explanation: Mild joke about Hilary's fitness level being intimidating. Not particularly memorable.

jokes.text: I've been canceled a time or two myself. Always led to something better.
jokes.setup: Will mentions being out of a job
jokes.punchline: Shatner's reference to his own career ups and downs
jokes.explanation: Nice meta-reference to Shatner's real career trajectory. Shows self-awareness and provides actual advice.

jokes.text: We move in different circles.
jokes.setup: Will asks if Shatner knows anyone who might hire him
jokes.punchline: Shatner's polite way of saying he can't help
jokes.explanation: Polite but firm rejection delivered with economy. Shows the class difference subtly.

jokes.text: How could he like you more than me? You haven't even read any of his books.
jokes.setup: Carlton is confused why Shatner prefers Will
jokes.punchline: Carlton's belief that reading someone's books should make them like you more
jokes.explanation: Carlton's naive belief that fandom should equal friendship. Shows his fundamental misunderstanding of celebrity interactions.

jokes.text: You broke my damn tooth.
jokes.setup: Physical altercation between Will and Carlton over pool cue
jokes.punchline: Shatner's injury from their fight
jokes.explanation: Standard physical comedy setup. The broken tooth creates the main plot complication.

jokes.text: Don't worry, Jim. We'll get you there warp speed. Get a life.
jokes.setup: Carlton tries to comfort injured Shatner with Star Trek reference
jokes.punchline: Shatner's famous 'Get a life' response to obsessive fans
jokes.explanation: Brilliant use of Shatner's actual famous 'Get a life' moment from SNL. Perfect timing and context make this a standout reference.

jokes.text: I can take it out on anyone I want. I'm William Shatner.
jokes.setup: Will argues Shatner shouldn't punish Hilary for their mistake
jokes.punchline: Shatner's arrogant declaration of his celebrity privilege
jokes.explanation: Perfect encapsulation of celebrity ego. The simple declaration 'I'm William Shatner' as justification for anything is both arrogant and funny.

jokes.text: Did you sense the power in that man?
jokes.setup: Shatner just delivered his arrogant ultimatum
jokes.punchline: Carlton's awed admiration for Shatner's rudeness
jokes.explanation: Carlton's continued worship of Shatner even when he's being awful. Shows his complete celebrity blindness.

jokes.text: Shut up.
jokes.setup: Carlton's admiration for Shatner's power
jokes.punchline: Will's exasperated response to Carlton's continued fandom
jokes.explanation: Simple reaction beat. Will's frustration is understandable but the joke is basic.

jokes.text: Carlton, he all hyped up on that goofy gas. Man, turn that thing off.
jokes.setup: Carlton is acting strange from nitrous oxide at the dentist
jokes.punchline: Will's realization and attempt to control the situation
jokes.explanation: Standard setup for drug-induced comedy. 'Goofy gas' is a nice term but overall predictable.

jokes.text: 'Hangding.' Carlton, he ain't snapping out of it, man.
jokes.setup: Will tries to get Carlton to snap out of his nitrous oxide high
jokes.punchline: Carlton's nonsensical 'hangding' response
jokes.explanation: Drug-induced nonsense word. Mildly amusing but relies on the cheap laughs of intoxication.

jokes.text: Hey, Bill, look. I'm Timmy the Tooth. Hi, Timmy. Wait, wait, wait. I wanna be Timmy. Let me be Timmy.
jokes.setup: Both Carlton and Shatner are high on nitrous oxide
jokes.punchline: Their childish argument over who gets to be 'Timmy the Tooth'
jokes.explanation: Drug-induced silliness. Seeing Shatner acting childish is mildly funny but relies heavily on intoxication humor.

jokes.text: Hey, you Timmy the tooth. I'm Bubba Gump.
jokes.setup: Will joins in the tooth-themed name game
jokes.punchline: Will's 'Bubba Gump' playing on both the tooth theme and Forrest Gump reference
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay combining the tooth theme with pop culture reference. Will joining the silliness works.

jokes.text: Hey, wait. Wait. Wait a second. Hey, Carlton, look. Uncle Phil's toothbrush.
jokes.setup: Will also gets affected by the nitrous gas
jokes.punchline: Will finding Uncle Phil's toothbrush hilarious while high
jokes.explanation: Standard drug humor escalation. Will getting high and finding mundane objects funny is predictable stoner comedy.

jokes.text: Spock, is that you? Your ears are the same but something's different.
jokes.setup: Shatner is high and seeing Will
jokes.punchline: Shatner thinks Will is Spock but notices he's Black
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist moment that plays with Star Trek while acknowledging racial differences in a lighthearted way.

jokes.text: Scotty, you're black.
jokes.setup: Shatner continues his Star Trek hallucination with Will
jokes.punchline: His matter-of-fact observation about Scotty's unexpected race change
jokes.explanation: The deadpan delivery of an obvious observation works. Simple but effective absurdist beat.

jokes.text: Damn it, Jim. I'm a black boy from Philly, not a doctor.
jokes.setup: Shatner asks 'Bones' to fix his tooth
jokes.punchline: Will's twist on Dr. McCoy's famous line incorporating his own identity
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback that perfectly adapts an iconic Star Trek quote to Will's character. The substitution works on multiple levels and becomes instantly quotable.

jokes.text: Throw your hands in the air if you's a true player
jokes.setup: Shatner is still high and acting like a rapper
jokes.punchline: His attempt at hip-hop vernacular and attitude
jokes.explanation: Shatner attempting hip-hop culture is absurd enough to be funny, though it relies on the incongruity.

jokes.text: This guy kills me. He's killing me.
jokes.setup: Shatner is enjoying his new hip-hop persona
jokes.punchline: Shatner praising himself in the third person while high
jokes.explanation: Self-referential humor. Shatner being impressed with his own performance while high shows his ego persisting even in altered states.

jokes.text: Hey, Werner, I just wanna say... I love you, man.
jokes.setup: Shatner is high and overly affectionate
jokes.punchline: His sincere declaration of love to a random person
jokes.explanation: Standard drug-induced affection. Mildly amusing but predictable behavior.

jokes.text: Sulu, where you been? And why are you out of uniform?
jokes.setup: Shatner continues seeing people as Star Trek characters
jokes.punchline: He thinks Geoffrey is Sulu and questions his civilian clothes
jokes.explanation: Continues the Star Trek hallucination theme. Functional but not particularly memorable.

jokes.text: Absolutely. I'll see you in court.
jokes.setup: Someone asks if Shatner loves them while he's high
jokes.punchline: His sudden shift to legal threats despite seeming agreement
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection. The 'Absolutely' sets up agreement but leads to a lawsuit threat. Good whiplash effect.

jokes.text: Everybody, rumba.
jokes.setup: Shatner is still high and commanding people around
jokes.punchline: Shatner ordering everyone to dance the rumba
jokes.explanation: Random but authoritative command. The specificity of 'rumba' rather than generic dancing makes it funnier and more Shatner-esque.

jokes.text: That's right, boy. You better recognize. ... No, no. You better not had meant, I know you ain't mean. Smarten up, Opie.
jokes.setup: Production assistant tries to manage high Shatner
jokes.punchline: Shatner's aggressive hip-hop attitude and 'Opie' insult
jokes.explanation: Shatner's continued hip-hop persona with the 'Opie' reference to Andy Griffith. Cultural clash humor works.

jokes.text: I may be in an altered state right now but when those cameras are on me... and those lights hit me, I'll be 100 percent dope.
jokes.setup: Concerns about Shatner being too high to perform
jokes.punchline: His confident declaration using '100 percent dope' slang
jokes.explanation: Great blend of professional confidence with drug-influenced slang. 'Dope' works as both 'good' and drug reference.

jokes.text: Girl, you a bad mamma jamma.
jokes.setup: Shatner's first words on Hilary's talk show
jokes.punchline: His inappropriate hip-hop greeting on live television
jokes.explanation: The inappropriateness of this on live TV makes it funnier. Shatner's drug-induced personality shift creates maximum cringe.

jokes.text: I know.
jokes.setup: Shatner calls Hilary a 'bad mamma jamma'
jokes.punchline: Hilary's matter-of-fact agreement with the compliment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary response. Her vanity makes her accept even inappropriate compliments as obvious truth.

jokes.text: Don't let your mouth write a check your pool stick can't cash.
jokes.setup: Pool challenge between Will and Shatner
jokes.punchline: Shatner's twist on the classic 'mouth writing checks' phrase
jokes.explanation: Clever variation on a classic phrase that fits the pool context perfectly. Good wordplay that enhances the original.

jokes.text: You never beat me at pool. / Oh, Bill, Bill, Bill. I see you still got your buzz on a little bit, huh?
jokes.setup: Shatner claiming he never lost at pool
jokes.punchline: Will's diplomatic way of acknowledging Shatner is still high
jokes.explanation: Good callback with diplomatic handling. Will's gentle acknowledgment of Shatner's continued intoxication while correcting the record.

jokes.text: And captain... live long and prosper.
jokes.setup: Will says goodbye to Shatner
jokes.punchline: Will uses Spock's famous Vulcan blessing to a surprised Shatner
jokes.explanation: Nice callback that shows Will learned something from the Star Trek obsession. The role reversal works well.

jokes.text: Hey, what about my autograph?
jokes.setup: Shatner is leaving after the reconciliation
jokes.punchline: Carlton still focused on getting his autograph after everything
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton priorities. Even after all the chaos, he's still focused on the autograph, showing his single-minded fan devotion.

jokes.text: Hey, what about my autograph?
jokes.setup: Everyone saying goodbye to Shatner
jokes.punchline: Carlton's persistent focus on getting his autograph despite everything that's happened
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton ending - despite all the chaos and Shatner's rudeness, he's still focused on his original goal. Never learns.

jokes.text: I've decided... Are you ready for this? ... to let you move to New York. But, Daddy... Honey, honey, let him have this one.
jokes.setup: Phil builds up to a big announcement about Ashley's future
jokes.punchline: His dramatic revelation that he's giving permission, followed by Vivian's deflation
jokes.explanation: Phil's need to make this dramatic reveal shows his character. Vivian's 'let him have this one' deflates his moment nicely.

jokes.text: Okay.
jokes.setup: Will gives inspirational speech about bouncing back from adversity
jokes.punchline: Hilary's final dismissive 'Okay' to his motivational moment
jokes.explanation: Perfect bookend to the running gag. Hilary's indifference to Will's inspirational speech maintains character consistency.

jokes.text: Adversification, but...
jokes.setup: Will is trying to sound profound about adversity
jokes.punchline: His made-up word 'adversification' reveals he's not as eloquent as he thinks
jokes.explanation: Will's attempt at sophisticated vocabulary backfires. The made-up word undermines his inspirational moment.

jokes.text: I lost my job, you lost your daughter. / Two daughters. / Two of them. Deuce. Deuce.
jokes.setup: Will and Phil commiserating about their losses
jokes.punchline: Will's casual 'deuce' reference treating the loss of daughters like a game score
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about loss. Will's sports terminology for Phil's family situation is inappropriately casual but funny in its tone-deafness.

jokes.text: I lost my job, you lost your daughter. Two daughters. Two of them. Deuce. Deuce.
jokes.setup: Will and Phil commiserating about their losses
jokes.punchline: Phil's correction about losing two daughters and the 'deuce' wordplay
jokes.explanation: Mild wordplay on 'two' and 'deuce.' The escalation from one to two daughters works but isn't groundbreaking.

jokes.text: Will you hold me?
jokes.setup: Phil and Will's bonding moment about their mutual losses
jokes.punchline: Phil's vulnerable request for physical comfort
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending that shows Phil's vulnerability beneath his authority. The request is both touching and awkward in the right way.
